title: ufget
html:
ufget

Get data from a field displayed in a Uniface component.

short ufget (unsigned char* field , unsigned char* buffer, short max );

Parameters

field
Name of the field. To resolve ambiguity, the name can be fully qualified in the format: field . entity.
buffer
(Output) buffer containing the field contents.
max
Size of the buffer pointed to by buffer. This value must not be larger than the actual size of the buffer.

Return Values

Table 1. Values returned by ufget and ufgetc
Value Description
>0 The number of bytes copied into the buffer
Zero An error occurred
<0 The negated number of bytes copied into the buffer. The negative status indicates that more data is available in the field and that you should call ufgetc to get that additional data.

Use

Library: yrtl

Description

ufget moves the data displayed in the field to the specified buffer. If there is more data in the field than the buffer can hold, ufget returns a negative status. In this case, you must call ufgetc repeatedly to get additional data until ufgetc returns a positive status.

Profile and subfield separator characters, if present in a field, are also returned in the buffer, so you should be prepared to handle these. For information about the representation of these characters, see the "Subfield Separator and Profile Characters" topic. .

Note: If you use both ufget and ufgetc for a single field, it is more efficient to use the internal bind name (returned by ugetbind ) instead of the actual field name.

The total length of field data returned by a sequence of ufget / ufgetc calls depends on the way the field is defined in the model and/or the component:

  • For a field defined in the application model, this length is the maximum of the lengths dictated by the packing code and the field syntax interface. For example, if a String field is defined with packing code C123 and syntax definition LEN(0-80), the total length returned will be 123.
  • For a field that is not defined in the application model but only in the component, the total length returned will be the length as specified in the syntax definition. If the field has no syntax definition, the total length returned will be the length as prescribed by the packing code.

For Unicode fields (defined with a W packing code), the field length is based on bytes, not characters. The maximum possible data size is caluclated using the system character set ($SYS_CHARSET). For example, if $SYS_CHARSET=UTF8, a W30 field is treated as a field with a capacity of 120 bytes because one UTF8 character takes up to 4 bytes.

Example: ufget and ufgetc

The following example illustrates how to use ufget, ufgetc, and ugetbind to read data from a specified (but arbitrary) field data into a buffer. Because the size of the field is unknown, the example reads fixed chunks and expands the buffer as necessary. The choice of 100 as the size of the fixed chunks is arbitrary.

#include <h3gl.h>
#define CHUNKSIZE 100
EXPORT void getfield (char* fldname)
{
char bindname[80];
char* buf;
int len;
int totlen;
ugetbind(fldname, bindname); /* Get bindname for efficiency */
buf = malloc(CHUNKSIZE); /* Allocate the buffer */
/* Get the first chunk of data */
len = ufget(bindname, buf, CHUNKSIZE);
totlen = abs(len); /* Set total length */
while ( len < 0 ) /* More to come */
{ /* Make room for next chunk */
buf = realloc(buf, totlen+CHUNKSIZE);
/* Get next chunk */
len = ufgetc(bindname, buf+totlen, CHUNKSIZE);
/* Update total length */
totlen += abs(len);
}
buf = realloc(buf, totlen+1); /* Make room for terminator */
buf[totlen] = 0; /* Add terminator */
}
Related Links
  • Subfield Separator and Profile Characters

    Uniface uses part of the reserved area of each meta character set (that is, the first 32 characters plus the last character) to store characters with special meaning within Uniface, such as subfield separators and profile characters.

  • ufgetc

    Get additional data from a field displayed in a Uniface component.

  • ugetbind

    Get the internal bind name of a Uniface field.

  • Data Storage

    Field interface definitions, as defined in the Database Interface property, determine how data is stored in a specific DBMS storage format, and how Uniface deals with the data when it is retrieved. The definitions specify the Uniface data type and packing code, which are mapped by the DBMS connector to the DBMS data type, known as a storage format.

  • $sys_charset

    Set or return the value of $SYS_CHARSET, which defines the character set used to communicate with components that are not Unicode-based, for example C components.
